title: Quiz: Ansatz Design
description: Test your understanding of quantum circuit ansatz design for VQE

questions.question: What is the primary role of an ansatz in VQE?

options.text: To compute the Hamiltonian
To prepare parameterized trial wavefunctions
To measure qubit states
To perform classical optimization

questions.explanation: The ansatz is a parameterized quantum circuit U(θ) that prepares trial wavefunctions |ψ(θ)⟩ = U(θ)|ψ₀⟩. VQE then optimizes these parameters to find the ground state.

questions.question: What is a barren plateau in variational quantum algorithms?

options.text: A region where the energy is constant
A region where gradients vanish exponentially with system size
A local minimum in the energy landscape
A hardware limitation

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus are regions where gradients ∂E/∂θ vanish exponentially as Var[∂E/∂θ] ∝ 1/2ⁿ, making optimization intractable for large systems with random initialization.

questions.question: What does UCCSD stand for?

options.text: Unitary Classical Coupled Single-Double
Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles
Universal Circuit for Chemical Simulation Design
Uniform Cluster Configuration States Distribution

questions.explanation: UCCSD stands for Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles, a chemistry-inspired ansatz that includes single and double excitation operators: |ψ⟩ = exp(T₁ + T₂ - h.c.)|HF⟩.

questions.question: How does ADAPT-VQE differ from fixed-structure VQE?

options.text: It uses a predetermined circuit structure
It grows the circuit adaptively based on gradients
It only works for hydrogen molecules
It doesn't require classical optimization

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE grows the quantum circuit adaptively by selecting operators from a pool based on which has the largest gradient magnitude, adding them one at a time until convergence.

questions.question: Hardware-efficient ansätze always outperform chemistry-inspired ansätze because they use native gates.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: False. While hardware-efficient ansätze use native gates and have lower per-layer depth, they often suffer from barren plateaus and may not represent the ground state as accurately as chemistry-inspired ansätze like UCCSD.

questions.question: What is expressibility in the context of quantum ansätze?

options.text: The ability to be compiled to native gates
The ability to represent a wide range of quantum states
The number of parameters in the circuit
The speed of circuit execution

questions.explanation: Expressibility measures how well an ansatz can cover the relevant Hilbert space. It quantifies how close the distribution of reachable states is to a Haar-random distribution.

questions.question: Why is the UCCSD ansatz naturally unitary?

options.text: Because it uses only single-qubit gates
Because exp(T - T†) is unitary when T - T† is anti-Hermitian
Because it operates on normalized states
Because coupled cluster theory requires it

questions.explanation: The operator (T - T†) is anti-Hermitian since (T - T†)† = T† - T = -(T - T†). The exponential of an anti-Hermitian operator is always unitary: exp(A)†exp(A) = I when A† = -A.

questions.question: Which are effective strategies to mitigate barren plateaus? (Select all)

options.text: Layer-wise training (growing circuit gradually)
Using deeper random circuits
Problem-specific parameter initialization
Using local cost functions when possible

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus can be mitigated by: (1) layer-wise training to avoid random deep circuits, (2) problem-specific initialization to start near good solutions, and (3) local cost functions that don't spread information globally. Deeper random circuits make barren plateaus worse.
